summary: User policy for the Spotlight collection option on the desktop background. Good for a more static personalization setup, but it removes a Microsoft-curated dynamic wallpaper option.

setting.name: Spotlight Collection on Desktop
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can offer a Spotlight collection option for desktop backgrounds. This policy decides whether that Spotlight wallpaper option is allowed for the current user.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it to keep desktop backgrounds predictable, stop dynamic Microsoft wallpaper content, or simplify personalization choices.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off reduces cloud-curated wallpaper content, but it also removes a built-in rotating desktop background option.
setting.professional_notes: The Experience Policy CSP uses the allow-form name AllowSpotlightCollection, while CloudContent.admx maps the user Group Policy to DisableSpotlightCollectionOnDesktop with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the desktop Spotlight collection policy for the current user.

allowed_values.label: Desktop Spotlight collection allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and the Spotlight collection wallpaper option remains available.

allowed_values.label: Desktop Spotlight collection disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The Spotlight collection wallpaper option is blocked by policy.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableSpotlightCollectionOnDesktop = 1, which matches the documented Group Policy disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows user-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 and Windows 11 client systems that support desktop Spotlight collection

states.rationale: The user policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: The Experience Policy CSP documents the allow-form default as allowed, while the registry policy baseline remains not configured unless the policy is set.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want rotating Microsoft-curated wallpapers
avoid_for: Users who prefer static wallpaper choices
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the desktop Spotlight collection option available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a static or fully manual desktop personalization setup.
tradeoffs: No policy-based restriction is enforced for this personalization option.

recommended_profiles.label: Static desktop
avoid_for: Users who enjoy Microsoft-curated changing wallpapers

states.rationale: Turn off the Spotlight collection wallpaper option through Group Policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to remove the Spotlight wallpaper option and keep desktop backgrounds more predictable.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want automatic Microsoft-curated wallpaper rotation.
tradeoffs: The Spotlight collection wallpaper option disappears.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: AllowSpotlightCollection
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the allow-form policy controlling the Spotlight collection desktop background option.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft CloudContent.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps the user Group Policy to DisableSpotlightCollectionOnDesktop with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 under the CloudContent policy key.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableSpotlightCollectionOnDesktop = 1 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft CloudContent policy files document the exact user-policy surface, numeric mapping, and desktop Spotlight behavior. The app writes the documented disabled value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableSpotlightCollectionOnDesktop" class="User" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CloudContent" valueName="DisableSpotlightCollectionOnDesktop">; CloudContent.adml: If you enable this policy, "Spotlight collection" will not be available as an option in Personalization settings.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact user-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy removes the Spotlight collection desktop option from Personalization settings.
